_comment: Maps agent design config. The Maps agent ASSEMBLES the tiles agent's isometric tiles (tiles/<category>/) into one hand-designed-feeling world. It generates no art of its own and needs no PixelLab key — building a world is pure compositing. See maps/README.md.
design_intent: An island kingdom, ALTTP-style: a single coherent landmass with an organic coastline, a sand beach ring, placed biome regions (northern stone highlands that rise in terraces, a snowy plateau, an eastern forest, fertile central plains), a river running from the peaks to the sea, and real landmarks — a walled castle on a plateau with a gate facing the plains, and a harbor town on the coast — tied together by logical A* roads that prefer flat, dry ground and bridge water only where they must. Nothing is scattered at random; every region has a place and a reason.

elevation._comment: Elevation is built by stacking the same 64x64 (50%-thickness) ground tiles, one tile-thickness per level (the tiles agent's documented method). Level 0 = ground/sea level.

biomes._comment: biome -> (tile category, preferred variant indices). Variants are a small curated set so a region reads as one material with natural internal variety, never a checkerboard.
biomes.snowfield: snow on a raised stone apron
biomes.castle: castle flagstone floor inside stone walls

iteration._comment: Each build.py --iterate applies ONE deliberate improvement, in priority order, then re-renders. The loop keeps making the world better/bigger.
steps: dock road from the town out to deep water
deepen the forest core into woodland
found a forest hamlet and road-link it
extend the world with a new coastal-plains frontier
